justification: All six pass criteria met: CQRS correctly identified with strong reasoning (read/write impedance mismatch, 500 writes/day vs heavy reads); read/write separation clearly designed with four specific denormalized views; eventual consistency explicitly stated and quantified (1-5 seconds, acceptable for operational dashboards); 50M record migration risk identified as high severity with concrete mitigation (backfill script); implementation_guidance is highly actionable with four phases, specific files, and pitfalls; CQRS explicitly limited to read path with repeated warnings not to add command buses or write-side CQRS.

observations.text: Explicitly rejected Event Sourcing with clear reasoning — avoids a key failure mode
Transactional Outbox pattern well-justified over CDC for the 500 writes/day scale
Four specific denormalized read model tables mapped to dashboard needs (OrdersByStatus, DeliverySuccessRates, TransitTimeByZone, CourierPerformance)
Phased implementation plan with shadow mode and feature flag rollback — de-risks migration
Pitfalls section explicitly warns against adding CQRS to the write path, skipping backfill, and over-engineering with Kafka
Reconciliation job proposed to detect projector drift — addresses a subtle operational risk
Eventual consistency quantified at 1-5 seconds with explanation of why this is acceptable for the use case
